title: The Wealth of Nations
author: Adam Smith
content: “It is not from the benevolence of the butcher, the brewer, or the baker that we expect our dinner, but from their regard to their own interest.”
action: Context: The recognition of self-interest as the primary engine for large-scale economic supply chains.

title: On War
author: Carl von Clausewitz
content: “Everything in war is very simple, but the simplest thing is difficult.”
action: Context: The delta between strategic planning and the execution of those plans in a high-entropy environment.

title: The Interpretation of Dreams
author: Sigmund Freud
content: “The interpretation of dreams is the royal road to a knowledge of the unconscious activities of the mind.”
action: Context: The assertion that non-linear, irrational thought is a data stream reflecting the deeper state of the system.

title: Social Contract
author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau
content: “Man is born free, and everywhere he is in chains.”
action: Context: The foundational tension between innate human state and the requirements of collective infrastructure.

title: Principia Mathematica
author: Isaac Newton
content: “If I have seen further it is by standing on the shoulders of Giants.”
action: Context: The acknowledgment that complex progress is cumulative, relying on the stability of existing foundations.
